description: A simple text-driven public-service awareness card on a plain white background framed by a thin black border with small notches at the upper-left and lower-right corners. In the top-left corner, the hashtag '#EndTrafficking' appears in blue. The central message is set in heavy bold sans-serif: 'Did You Know?' in black, followed by the statement 'Human trafficking victims often know the person who lures them into a trafficking situation.' in bright blue. The lower-right corner carries the DHS Blue Campaign logo (a stylized blue circular swirl) with the wordmark 'BLUE CAMPAIGN' and the tagline 'One Voice. One Mission. End Human Trafficking.' This is an official DHS Blue Campaign awareness graphic (not a meme), using minimalist typographic design and a fact/'Did You Know?' framing to educate the public about how human trafficking recruitment works. Intent is educational/awareness; target audience is the general public and potential trafficking victims.
notable_text: #EndTrafficking; Did You Know? Human trafficking victims often know the person who lures them into a trafficking situation.; BLUE CAMPAIGN - One Voice. One Mission. End Human Trafficking.
